p.seg: To all the sons of holy mother Church present and to come, John, son of Michael of Methil sends greeting.

Let your whole community know me to have given, granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and the church of St Mary at Melrose, and the monks in that place who serve God now and in the future, my whole land at Penshiels; for the said monks to have and to hold of me and my heirs in pure and everlasting alms for ever, as freely, quitely, fully and honourably as any alms in the kingdom of Scotland are freely, quitely and fully held and possessed; in wood and plain, in lands cultivated and uncultivated, in . . . , in roads and paths, in moors, peat-bogs, and marshes, in running waters and pools, fish-ponds and mills, and . . . easements which can be found or exercised within the foresaid land; by paying thereupon yearly at Whitsunday to the the one who is prior of Melrose at the time three merks of silver, which for the salvation of my soul and the souls of my heirs I have assigned for purchasing a pittance annually on the day of the Holy Trinity, to the monastic community of Melrose, to be dispensed by the hands of the said prior. And I and my heirs shall maintain and defend to the foresaid monks the whole land aforesaid, with all its liberties and just pertinents, against all men; excepting the lands which I have given to the monks of May, and excepting the land which I have allowed to Henry Little and his heirs, by their correct boundaries, in the territory of [Pen]shiels. But the whole land aforesaid, as it is said, the said monks of Melrose shall hold and have . . . and quit from all service, custom, exaction and demand of me and my heirs for ever, doing as aforesaid.

With these witnesses: the lord Alan, abbot of St Edward's at Balmerino; Thomas, earl of Atholl; Walter, son of Alan, steward of the lord king, W., his chaplain; Thomas of Kent; Master John 'Abigensi'; Mastcr Ness, physician of the lord king; Malcolm, son of the earl of Lennox; Geoffrey del Bois; James, son of William; John Comyn; Andrew of Swinton; and many others.
